title: My Opposites Book & Activities
description: A vocabulary pack for K-2 ELL newcomers focusing on common opposite words in English and Spanish, with visual aids and interactive activities.

vocabulary.image_prompt: an ice cube or a snowman
an arrow pointing upwards, a balloon floating up
an arrow pointing downwards, a ball falling down
a dark sky with a moon and stars
a frowning face emoji with a tear

worksheets.instructions_en: Draw a line from the word on the left to its opposite word on the right.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Dibuja una línea de la palabra de la izquierda a su palabra opuesta en la derecha.

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the words and say them aloud.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras y dilas en voz alta.

items.image_prompt: a big elephant outline for tracing
a small mouse outline for tracing
an ice cream cone outline for tracing
a balloon flying up outline for tracing

worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea los dibujos como se describe.

items.content_l2: Colorea el elefante GRANDE de azul.
items.image_prompt: outline drawing of a big elephant

items.content_l2: Colorea el ratón PEQUEÑO de rojo.
items.image_prompt: outline drawing of a small mouse

items.content_l2: Colorea el sol CALIENTE de amarillo.
items.image_prompt: outline drawing of a smiling sun

items.content: Color the COLD ice cream white.
items.content_l2: Colorea el helado FRÍO de blanco.
items.image_prompt: outline drawing of an ice cream cone

items.content_l2: Colorea la cara FELIZ de verde.
items.image_prompt: outline drawing of a happy face

items.content_l2: Colorea la cara TRISTE de morado.
items.image_prompt: outline drawing of a sad face

pages.image_prompt: A cheerful book cover with two opposite images, like a big and small object.

pages.text_en:
Big / Small
A big elephant. A small mouse.
pages.text_l2:
Grande / Pequeño
Un elefante grande. Un ratón pequeño.
pages.image_prompt: a big elephant and a small mouse side by side

pages.text_en:
Hot / Cold
A hot sun. Cold ice cream.
pages.text_l2:
Caliente / Frío
Un sol caliente. Un helado frío.
pages.image_prompt: a hot sun and an ice cream cone

pages.text_en:
Up / Down
The balloon goes up. The ball goes down.
pages.text_l2:
Arriba / Abajo
El globo sube. La pelota baja.
pages.image_prompt: a balloon floating up and a ball bouncing down

pages.text_en:
Day / Night
It is day. It is night.
pages.text_l2:
Día / Noche
Es de día. Es de noche.
pages.image_prompt: a sunny landscape and a starry night landscape

pages.text_en:
Happy / Sad
A happy face. A sad face.
pages.text_l2:
Feliz / Triste
Una cara feliz. Una cara triste.
pages.image_prompt: a happy emoji and a sad emoji

pages.text_en:
Open / Close
Open the door. Close the door.
pages.text_l2:
Abrir / Cerrar
Abre la puerta. Cierra la puerta.
pages.image_prompt: an open door and a closed door

pages.text_en:
Fast / Slow
A fast car. A slow turtle.
pages.text_l2:
Rápido / Lento
Un coche rápido. Una tortuga lenta.
pages.image_prompt: a race car speeding and a turtle slowly walking

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify, understand, and use common opposite words (e.g., big/small, hot/cold) in English and Spanish.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary with flashcards and gestures. Read the mini-book aloud, pointing to pictures. Use the tracing worksheet for handwriting practice and word recognition. The matching worksheet reinforces understanding of opposites. The coloring worksheet provides a fun way to apply vocabulary and follow instructions. Encourage students to create their own sentences using the opposite words.
differentiation_tips: **For emerging learners:** Provide extra visual support and gestures. Use realia (real objects) when possible. Focus on 2-3 pairs at a time. Allow students to point instead of speaking.

**For developing learners:** Encourage students to repeat words and phrases. Provide sentence frames (e.g., 'The elephant is _____. The mouse is _____.') for oral practice. Partner work for matching activities.
**For expanding learners:** Challenge students to use the words in original sentences. Ask 'why' questions (e.g., 'Why is the sun hot?'). Have them draw their own opposite pairs.

answers.4: Ice cream should be colored white.
answers.5: Happy face should be colored green.
answers.6: Sad face should be colored purple.
